title: DIY Modern Cedar Planter
summary: Building a spot for your very own Garden of Eden shouldn't feel like a hard task.
description: Building a spot for your very own Garden of Eden shouldn't feel like a hard task. Learn how to build this easy Modern Cedar Planter in just a few steps!

steps.title: Check the Dimensions
steps.body: Make sure to check the dimensions and make any necessary adjustments before starting.

steps.title: Cut Your Pieces
steps.body: All of the pieces used for the sides of this planter have 45 degree beveled ends. Make sure to double check the dimensions required prior to cutting your ends.

steps.title: Attach the Long Side Pieces
steps.body: Attach the large side pieces in sets of two with wood glue, pocket holes, and screws.

steps.title: Attach the Short Side Pieces
steps.body: Attach the short sides the same way.

steps.title: Build Bottom Piece
steps.body: Build the bottom piece with pocket holes and wood glue. Make sure there are pocket holes along the outside of the piece for attaching to the sides.

steps.title: Attach Bottom Piece
steps.body: Attach the bottom piece to all four sides with wood glue and pocket hole screws. You may want to drill small holes in the bottom for drainage.

steps.title: Add Corner Supports
steps.body: Add the four small corner supports to the inside corners. Attach them from the outside of the planter by shooting 1" brad nails into each support. This will help keep the beveled corners tight. Add wood glue for extra strength.

steps.title: Sand & Seal
steps.body: Fill your pocket holes, nail holes, and corners (if there are gaps). Then sand & seal your planter!

steps.title: Do Some Planting
steps.body: Make sure to add some plants to your awesome new build!
